title: Getting started with the app
text: Start telling DataGangeR synthetic data . Instead separate Fidelity / Privacy / Anonymity meters, app now shows one Protection meter: Demo / Teaching = 5 bars, Development prototyping = 3 bars, Internal Analytics = 1 bar. Development default objective. click objective, detail panel explains along dimensions every time: Use , Exact values, Distributions, Relationships, Identifiers, Sensitive & rare values. keeps trade-readable without making interpret multiple competing meters.

title: Getting started with the app
text: Drop CSV, Excel, SAS file — load one built-sample datasets explore workflow without real data. right-hand panel previews data live loads.

title: Getting started with the app
text: , human, make decisions. Nothing generated reviewed . screen built around privacy-first column classification, plus synthesis settings described .  Per-column decisions. row now asks two intrinsic questions: — blood pressure, lab value, score, price, outcome combined columns — age, sex, ZIP / postcode, birth date, job title Yes, directly — name, email, phone, address, Social Security number, record / account number Yes — mark Yes values like diagnosis, income, religion, anything cause harm linked back person choose action separately normal flow. DataGangeR derives two answers. Configure, per-column Action override cell exposes optional Action Data type overrides directly need . plain-English outcome reviewed Generate step: Direct identifiers always drop. Everything non-direct synthesized. Every column still needs explicit answer first question can generate. Synthesis settings. objective picked presets safe defaults, can override : Defaults safe objective picked — leave unless reason change . control app also carries one-line explanation, every decision recorded exported spec result reproducible (see synth_spec() programmatic equivalents).

title: Getting started with the app
text: anything generated, step shows per-column review table mirrors Configure choices spells outcome: two questions answered (Points person? Sensitive?), resulting Action (synthesize, pass , drop), plain-English ’ll column. Hover column name see modelled. anything looks wrong, use ← Adjust settings go back; otherwise generate synthetic double. summary reports row column counts, engine used, elapsed time, right panel previews synthetic records.

title: Getting started with the app
text: Inspect faithfully synthetic data mirrors original. Pick variable see overlaid distributions (green original data, magenta synthetic), Q–Q plot, side--side statistics table delta .

title: Getting started with the app
text: -app download compact bundle: small, shareable package built around six files: synthetic_data.csv README.md load_data.R analysis.qmd data_dictionary.csv manifest.json rendered comparison included, also get comparison_report.html. compact bundle, README.md consolidated: includes privacy summary AI assistants section, separate ai-readme.md privacy_report.txt. standalone files stay full CLI / agent bundle (compact = FALSE make_agent_bundle()), along extra diagnostics used agent workflows.  Remember: synthetic data reduces direct disclosure risk; replace formal privacy assessment. Review comparison privacy warnings sharing output externally.

title: DataGangeR internal consistency audit

Current behavior: mod_roles.R counts unset identifies values renders: N columns still need answer can generate. observeEvent(input$confirm, ...) still confirms roles without checking whether eligible column unanswered. mod_generate.R starts generation state$raw_data state$spec exist; validate whether state$roles complete. run_synthesis_pipeline() also validate role completeness. contradicts human-gated privacy model. also means user can see warning saying generation blocked backend still permits generation.

previous_headings: P0: Configure gate is UI-only; generation can proceed with unanswered privacy questions
title: DataGangeR internal consistency audit
text: Add single internal helper, example: enforce relevant paths: mod_roles.R confirm button: increment roles_confirmed ready; show notification. mod_generate.R generate/regenerate path: refuse generation ready. run_synthesis_pipeline() synthesize_data() called app roles: abort roles incomplete disclosure axes unless explicitly bypassed. Prefer placing pipeline/app layer scripted API remains flexible, add argument needed.
code: roles_ready_for_generation <- function(roles) {   if (is.null(roles) || !"identifies" %in% names(roles)) return(FALSE)   eligible <- rep(TRUE, nrow(roles))   if ("simulation" %in% names(roles)) {     eligible <- !(roles$simulation %in% c("drop", "pass_through"))     eligible[is.na(eligible)] <- TRUE   }   all(!(is.na(roles$identifies[eligible]) | !nzchar(roles$identifies[eligible]))) }

what: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: DataGangeR creates synthetic data doubles real datasets can prototype code, build Shiny apps, teach, collaborate AI tools without sharing original dataset. 📖 Documentation: https://lennon-li.github.io/dataganger/

what: Want AI to prototype on your data — without handing over the real thing?
title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: DataGangeR turns sensitive datasets safe, synthetic stand-ins. AI assistant prototypes full speed; real people data never leave building. 🤖 Harness AI, keep privacy — feed agents realistic synthetic data via built-CLI, production records. 🧭 Human control — guided UI lets decide ’s identifying, sensitive, safe, column column. ⚙️ Real synthesis — synthpop + k-anonymity: faithful enough build , private design. 🔁 Reproducible & human-gated — every export one-click bundle R script regenerate exact result. Prototype AI. Protect data. .

title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: Analysts often need share data structure teammates, students, AI assistants. Sharing original data always possible. DataGangeR generates synthetic “doppelganger” preserves structure, distributions, relationships need development reducing need expose original records. Important: Synthetic data intended reduce direct disclosure risk, replace formal privacy assessment. Review comparison privacy warnings sharing output externally.

title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
code: # Development version from GitHub: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("lennon-li/dataganger")

title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: guided Shiny app takes real dataset shareable synthetic bundle six steps — pick objective, upload data (load built-sample), configure answering two questions per column (point person? sensitive?) reviewing DataGangeR , generate synthetic double, compare real vs. synthetic distributions, export bundle. sidebar also includes Report problem button opens pre-filled GitHub issue browser.
code: library(dataganger) run_app()

title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: Every step app performs plain function call, can script whole pipeline without UI. Objective presets : development (default) — balanced protection prototyping app development demo — strongest protection, best teaching low-risk sharing analytics — highest fidelity, emphasis preserving relationships end--end reproducible pipeline looks like : Shiny app downloads compact bundle humans. CLI agent workflows, use full bundle (compact = FALSE, make_agent_bundle()) standalone ai-readme.md, privacy_report.txt, diagnostics included alongside analysis.qmd.
code: library(dataganger)  dat     <- read_input("my-data.csv")          # or: individual_sample profile <- profile_data(dat) roles   <- detect_roles(dat, profile) spec    <- synth_spec(purpose = "development", roles = roles, seed = 42) syn     <- synthesize_data(dat, spec, roles) export_synthetic(   syn,   original = dat,   path = "dataganger_bundle.zip",   compact = FALSE )  unzip("dataganger_bundle.zip", exdir = "dataganger_bundle")  # Re-run the same-seed audit/comparison notebook against the exported bundle. quarto::quarto_render(   "dataganger_bundle/analysis.qmd",   execute_params = list(     original_path = "my-data.csv",     synthetic_path = "dataganger_bundle/synthetic_data.csv"   ) )  # Open a pre-filled issue for bugs, feedback, or feature requests report_issue("The compare step was hard to interpret", context = "Shiny app")

previous_headings: Use it from R
title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: CLI follows spec-first pipeline agent use: unzip full bundle render included notebook real input file reproduce review -seed result: full command list, run dataganger::dataganger_cli(c("--help")).
code: dataganger profile my-data.csv --out profile.json dataganger roles my-data.csv --out roles.yaml dataganger spec --purpose development --out spec.yaml  # Edit spec.yaml if needed: set seed, engine/name_strategy overrides, # and disclosure_roles: <column>: <direct|quasi|sensitive|none>. dataganger synthesize my-data.csv --spec spec.yaml --out dataganger_bundle.zip dataganger inspect dataganger_bundle.zip unzip dataganger_bundle.zip -d dataganger_bundle quarto render dataganger_bundle/analysis.qmd \   -P original_path:my-data.csv \   -P synthetic_path:dataganger_bundle/synthetic_data.csv

title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: DataGangeR uses two synthesis engines. default engine chosen automatically objective: lower-fidelity objectives use dependency-free internal marginal engine, analytics purpose - high-fidelity setting, preserving relationships variables matters - uses synthpop package (Nowok, Raab & Dibben, 2016). Shiny app can also choose engine explicitly (auto, internal, synthpop). Install install.packages("synthpop") enable relationship-preserving synthesis full fidelity. Please cite synthpop use engine: Nowok B, Raab GM, Dibben C (2016). “synthpop: Bespoke Creation Synthetic Data R.” Journal Statistical Software, 74(11), 1-26. doi:10.18637/jss.v074.i11

title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping
text: Package-first. core functions work R console; Shiny optional interface layer. Configurable disclosure posture. synthesis purpose (development, demo, analytics, etc.) applies appropriate defaults coarsening, name handling, rare-level treatment. Honest comparisons. comparison report quantifies closely synthetic data mirrors original can make informed sharing decision. overclaims. DataGangeR tell output safe public release. determination depends data, context, applicable regulations.

title: DataGangeR — Synthetic Data Doubles for Safer Prototyping

what: Assess k-anonymity over a set of quasi-identifier columns — assess_kanonymity
title: Assess k-anonymity over a set of quasi-identifier columns — assess_kanonymity
text: Cross-tabulates quasi-identifier columns reports many records fall combinations (equivalence classes) smaller k. NA treated distinct level missing values mask small cell.

title: Assess k-anonymity over a set of quasi-identifier columns — assess_kanonymity
code: assess_kanonymity(data, qi_cols, k = 5)

title: Assess k-anonymity over a set of quasi-identifier columns — assess_kanonymity
text: data data frame. qi_cols Character vector quasi-identifier column names. k Minimum acceptable cell size (default 5).

title: Assess k-anonymity over a set of quasi-identifier columns — assess_kanonymity
text: list no_qi (logical), smallest_cell (integer), n_below, pct_below, worst_cells (tibble smallest combinations counts).

what: Check whether synthetic data is code-compatible with the original — check_code_readiness
title: Check whether synthetic data is code-compatible with the original — check_code_readiness
text: Evaluates whether code written synthetic development twin run original data without errors. Checks column presence, R class compatibility, factor level coverage, -NA columns, zero-variance columns, missingness spikes, ID uniqueness.

title: Check whether synthetic data is code-compatible with the original — check_code_readiness
code: check_code_readiness(original, synthetic, roles = NULL)

title: Check whether synthetic data is code-compatible with the original — check_code_readiness
text: original original data frame. synthetic synthetic data frame (synthesize_data() make_agent_bundle()). roles Optional; dataganger_roles object detect_roles(). Used ID-uniqueness checks. Computed internally NULL.

title: Check whether synthetic data is code-compatible with the original — check_code_readiness
text: S3 object class dataganger_code_readiness components: checks tibble one row per check: check, scope, column, status ("pass"/"warn"/"fail"), message. summary List n_pass, n_warn, n_fail, ready (TRUE n_fail == 0). meta List dimensions generated_at.

title: Check whether synthetic data is code-compatible with the original — check_code_readiness
code: orig <- data.frame(x = 1:10, y = factor(letters[1:10])) spec <- synth_spec(purpose = "demo") syn  <- synthesize_data(orig, spec) check_code_readiness(orig, syn) #>  #> ── DataGangeR Code Readiness ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 9 pass, 1 warn, 1 fail #> ✖ Not ready: 1 blocking issue #>  #>  #> ── Failures ── #>  #> ✖ [x] class mismatch: original 'integer' vs synthetic 'numeric' #>  #> ── Warnings ── #>  #> ! [y] Synthetic column has <= 1 unique value; model formulas using this column may fail

what: Compare original and synthetic datasets — compare_synthetic
title: Compare original and synthetic datasets — compare_synthetic
text: Compares original dataset synthetic double across dataset-level dimensions, numeric distributions, categorical distributions, numeric correlations. Returns structured dataganger_comparison object.

title: Compare original and synthetic datasets — compare_synthetic
code: compare_synthetic(original, synthetic, roles = NULL)

title: Compare original and synthetic datasets — compare_synthetic
text: original original data frame. synthetic synthetic data frame (synthesize_data()). roles Optional; dataganger_roles object detect_roles().

title: Compare original and synthetic datasets — compare_synthetic
text: S3 object class dataganger_comparison, list components dataset, numeric, categorical, relationship, privacy_flags, meta.

title: Compare original and synthetic datasets — compare_synthetic
code: dat <- data.frame(x = 1:10, y = letters[1:10]) spec <- synth_spec(purpose = "demo") syn <- synthesize_data(dat, spec) compare_synthetic(dat, syn) #> ℹ Not enough numeric columns (1) for correlation comparison. #>   Need at least 2 numeric columns with non-zero variance. #>  #> ── DataGangeR Comparison ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Dataset ── #>  #> • Rows: 10 (original) -> 10 (synthetic) #> • Columns: 2 (original) -> 2 (synthetic) #> • Type match: 50% #> • Missing: 0% (original) -> 0% (synthetic) #>  #> ── Numeric -- top 3 by |standardized difference| ── #>  #> • x: std diff = -0.727 #> Orig mean (SD): 5.5 (3.03) #>  #> ── Categorical -- top 3 by total variation distance ── #>  #> • y: TVD = 1 #> Levels: 10 (orig) -> 1 (syn)

what: DataGangeR uses two synthesis engines. By default the engine is chosen automatically from your objective: the analytics purpose (and any high-fidelity setting), where preserving relationships between variables matters, uses the synthpop package (Nowok, Raab & Dibben, 2016); other objectives use a dependency-free internal marginal engine. In the Shiny app the engine can also be selected explicitly (auto, internal, or synthpop). Install synthpop with install.packages(
title: DataGangeR uses two synthesis engines. By default the engine is chosen automatically from your objective: the analytics purpose (and any high-fidelity setting), where preserving relationships between variables matters, uses the synthpop package (Nowok, Raab & Dibben, 2016); other objectives use a dependency-free internal marginal engine. In the Shiny app the engine can also be selected explicitly (auto, internal, or synthpop). Install synthpop with install.packages(
text: synthpop used, please cite: Nowok B, Raab GM, Dibben C (2016). "synthpop: Bespoke Creation Synthetic Data R." Journal Statistical Software, 74(11), 1-26. doi:10.18637/jss.v074.i11

title: DataGangeR uses two synthesis engines. In the Shiny app the engine can also be selected explicitly (auto, internal, or synthpop). Install synthpop with install.packages(

title: DataGangeR command-line interface — dataganger_cli
code: dataganger_cli(args = commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE), quit = FALSE)

title: DataGangeR command-line interface — dataganger_cli
text: args Character vector trailing command-line arguments. quit Logical. TRUE, terminate R process using returned status code. Tests pass FALSE assert integer code.

what: Detect data roles for each column — detect_roles
title: Detect data roles for each column — detect_roles
text: Applies heuristic-based role detection every column data frame. Roles include ID candidate, date, label_check (labelled vectors), categorical candidate, free text, unknown. assignments overridable passing user_role column supplied roles tibble.

title: Detect data roles for each column — detect_roles
code: detect_roles(data, profile = NULL)

title: Detect data roles for each column — detect_roles
text: data data frame. profile Optional; dataganger_profile object profile_data(). NULL (default), profiling performed internally.

title: Detect data roles for each column — detect_roles
text: S3 object class dataganger_roles, tibble columns: variable Column name. class R class column. recommended_role Role detected heuristic. user_role User-supplied override (initially NA). simulation column treated synthesis. reason Justification recommended role. disclosure_role Disclosure role. NA (unselected) conservative default whenever detection confident; user must choose role generating. "direct" "sensitive" values auto-assigned (confident identifier / known-sensitive name). "quasi" "none" user-assigned choices . disclosure_reason Justification auto-assigned disclosure role.

title: Detect data roles for each column — detect_roles
code: df <- data.frame(   id   = 1:50,   date = as.Date("2020-01-01") + 0:49,   city = rep(c("Toronto", "Vancouver", "Montreal"), length.out = 50),   cat  = factor(rep(letters[1:3], length.out = 50)) ) detect_roles(df) #>  #> ── DataGangeR Roles ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 4 columns analysed; 0 user overrides active #>  #>  #> ── id (numeric) -> ID candidate  #> • Reason: The column name suggests an identifier, such as an ID, record number, #> or key. #> • Disclosure: direct #>  #> ── date (Date) -> date  #> • Reason: Stored as a date/time value, so it is treated as a date column. #> • Disclosure: quasi #>  #> ── city (character) -> categorical candidate  #> • Reason: Only a few distinct values appear, so this looks like a coded #> category rather than a measurement. #>  #> ── cat (factor) -> categorical candidate  #> • Reason: Only a few distinct values appear, so this looks like a coded #> category rather than a measurement. #>

what: Enforce k-anonymity on a synthetic dataset (output guarantee) — enforce_kanon
title: Enforce k-anonymity on a synthetic dataset (output guarantee) — enforce_kanon
text: Shapes synthetic output quasi-identifier combination appears fewer k records. Direct identifiers removed. Quasi-identifiers coarsened step--step residual cell still k QI values blanked (NA). Operates output .

title: Enforce k-anonymity on a synthetic dataset (output guarantee) — enforce_kanon
code: enforce_kanon(synthetic, roles, k = 5, max_steps = 6L, max_suppress_frac = 0.2)

title: Enforce k-anonymity on a synthetic dataset (output guarantee) — enforce_kanon
text: synthetic synthetic data frame. roles roles object/data frame variable + disclosure_role. k Minimum cell size (default 5). max_steps Maximum coarsening iterations (default 6). max_suppress_frac Feasibility backstop. satisfying k quasi-identifier set require blanking fraction rows, k-anonymity treated infeasible chosen QI set: coarsening/suppression applied (destroy dataset), synthetic output returned populated, warning advises narrowing quasi-identifiers lowering k. Default 0.2.

title: Enforce k-anonymity on a synthetic dataset (output guarantee) — enforce_kanon
text: shaped synthetic data frame, attribute kanon recording achieved state (smallest_cell, suppressed_cells, qi_cols, k, infeasible).

what: Export a synthetic data bundle — export_synthetic
title: Export a synthetic data bundle — export_synthetic
text: Writes reviewable export bundle containing synthetic data, data dictionary, comparison report, privacy report, manifest, analysis.qmd Quarto report (R code compare synthetic data original), helper files re-loading bundle. standalone data dictionary file optional via include_dictionary. default bundle written zip archive.

title: Export a synthetic data bundle — export_synthetic
code: export_synthetic(   synthetic,   original = NULL,   comparison = NULL,   privacy = NULL,   path,   format = c("zip", "dir"),   sanitize_for_spreadsheets = TRUE,   purpose = NULL,   roles = NULL,   include_original_names = NULL,   fail_on_exact_match = FALSE,   include_report = TRUE,   include_dictionary = TRUE,   code_readiness = NULL,   compact = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Export a synthetic data bundle — export_synthetic
text: synthetic synthetic data frame, typically synthesize_data(). original Optional original data frame. provided, used data dictionary, comparison fallback, privacy fallback, exact-row guard. comparison Optional dataganger_comparison object. NULL original supplied, compare_synthetic() run automatically. privacy Optional dataganger_privacy_check object. NULL original supplied, privacy_check() run automatically post stage. path Output path. Required. format = "zip", archive path. format = "dir", output directory. format Character. One "zip" "dir". sanitize_for_spreadsheets Logical. TRUE (default), character-like cells beginning =, +, -, @ leading whitespace prefixed single quote CSV export. purpose Optional purpose label README text. Defaults purpose recorded attr(synthetic, "spec") available. roles Optional recorded role decisions dataganger_roles data frame. supplied, export bundle includes exact column decisions needed reproduce synthetic output. include_original_names Logical NULL. Controls whether data_dictionary.csv includes original variable names. NULL, defaults TRUE unless name_strategy = "dictionary_only", case defaults FALSE. fail_on_exact_match Logical. TRUE, abort export exact-row matches detected nrow(original) >= 20. FALSE (default), exact-row matches recorded privacy report manifest, warning emitted instead. include_report Logical. TRUE (default), write comparison_report.html. rmarkdown/knitr unavailable, report skipped message instead error. include_dictionary Logical. TRUE (default), write data_dictionary.csv bundle. dictionary always computed memory load helper README; controls whether standalone CSV written. code_readiness Optional dataganger_code_readiness object check_code_readiness(). supplied, writes code_readiness_report.json bundle. compact Logical. TRUE, produce smaller bundle folding AI summary privacy report README.md instead also writing standalone ai-readme.md privacy_report.txt. README always contains information; controls extra files. Defaults FALSE (full bundle). overwrite Logical. FALSE (default), existing output paths refused.

title: Export a synthetic data bundle — export_synthetic
text: Invisibly, written bundle path.

title: Export a synthetic data bundle — export_synthetic
code: dat <- data.frame(id = 1:50, grp = rep(letters[1:5], each = 10)) spec <- synth_spec(purpose = "demo", seed = 1) syn <- synthesize_data(dat, spec) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ export_synthetic(syn, original = dat, path = tempfile(fileext = ".zip")) } # }

title: Plot comparison summaries — plot_comparison

title: Plot comparison summaries — plot_comparison
code: plot_comparison(comparison)

title: Plot comparison summaries — plot_comparison
text: comparison dataganger_comparison object compare_synthetic().

title: Plot comparison summaries — plot_comparison
text: Invisibly, list two ggplot objects: numeric categorical. NULL columns type exist.

title: Plot comparison summaries — plot_comparison
code: dat <- data.frame(x = 1:10, y = letters[1:10]) spec <- synth_spec(purpose = "demo") syn <- synthesize_data(dat, spec) cmp <- compare_synthetic(dat, syn) #> ℹ Not enough numeric columns (1) for correlation comparison. #>   Need at least 2 numeric columns with non-zero variance. if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   plot_comparison(cmp) }

title: Run disclosure-risk privacy checks — privacy_check
text: Scans original (optionally) synthetic data disclosure-risk flags. Supports two stages: "pre" (synthesis, requires original dataset roles) "post" (synthesis, requires original synthetic).

title: Run disclosure-risk privacy checks — privacy_check
code: privacy_check(   original,   synthetic = NULL,   roles = NULL,   stage = c("pre", "post"),   spec = NULL )

title: Run disclosure-risk privacy checks — privacy_check
text: original original data frame. synthetic Optional; synthetic data frame (required stage = "post"). roles Optional; dataganger_roles object detect_roles(). Required pre-stage flag detection. stage Character. "pre" "post". spec Optional; dataganger_spec object. provided stage = "post", cross-checks synthesis parameters applied (e.g. date coarsening, ID removal).

title: Run disclosure-risk privacy checks — privacy_check
text: S3 object class dataganger_privacy_check, tibble columns variable, flag, severity, stage, recommendation.

title: Run disclosure-risk privacy checks — privacy_check
code: df <- data.frame(id = 1:50, x = rnorm(50), city = rep("Toronto", 50)) roles <- detect_roles(df) privacy_check(df, roles = roles, stage = "pre") #>  #> ── DataGangeR Privacy Check (pre stage) ──────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── x HIGH severity (1) ── #>  #> • id: ID column detected #> Recommendation: Review whether this column should be excluded from synthetic #> output #>  #> ── i LOW severity (1) ── #>  #> • city: Geography column detected #> Recommendation: Geography columns can be re-identifying; consider coarsening or #> aggregation

code: profile_data(data)

title: Profile a dataset column-by-column — profile_data
code: df <- data.frame(   id = 1:5,   name = letters[1:5],   score = c(10.1, 15.2, NA, 13.8, 11.0) ) profile_data(df) #>  #> ── DataGangeR Profile ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 5 rows x 3 columns #>  #> ── Column types ── #>  #> • character: 1 #> • numeric: 2 #>  #> ── Missingness summary ── #>  #> Total missing: 1 / 15 (6.7%) #>  #> ── Per-column details ── #>  #> ── id (numeric)  #> • Missing: 0 (0%) #> • Distinct values: 5 #> • Range: [1, 5] #> • Mean (SD): 3 (1.58) #> • Median (IQR): 3 (2 -- 4) #>  #> ── name (character)  #> • Missing: 0 (0%) #> • Distinct values: 5 #> • Mean char length: 1 #>  #> ── score (numeric)  #> • Missing: 1 (20%) #> • Distinct values: 4 #> • Range: [10.1, 15.2] #> • Mean (SD): 12.53 (2.38) #> • Median (IQR): 12.4 (10.78 -- 14.15) #>  #> ℹ Generated at 2026-06-29 02:24:24.392895

title: Read a data file into a tibble — read_input

Passed readr::locale(encoding = encoding). Ignored reading non-CSV formats caller already supplies locale argument .... ... Additional arguments passed underlying reader (readr::read_csv(), readxl::read_excel(), haven::read_sas(), haven::read_xpt()).

what: Suggest a sufficient synthetic row count — suggest_min_rows
title: Suggest a sufficient synthetic row count — suggest_min_rows
text: Given profile_data() profile (carries cross-column coverage information), suggests many rows synthesize synthetic data can still represent every category combination every category level observed original data, without blindly matching large original row count.

title: Suggest a sufficient synthetic row count — suggest_min_rows
code: suggest_min_rows(   profile,   roles = NULL,   data = NULL,   k = 5L,   threshold = 1000L,   cap = 5000L )

title: Suggest a sufficient synthetic row count — suggest_min_rows
text: profile dataganger_profile profile_data(). roles Optional; dataganger_roles object. provided together data, coverage computation filtered columns whose effective role synthesizable (excludes ID candidates, free text, user-excluded columns). data Optional; original data frame. provided alongside roles, coverage recomputed filtered column subset suggestion reacts role changes Configure page. k Reserved future k-anonymity-style cell-size floor; unused current coverage rule. threshold Row count reduction suggested. cap Maximum suggested row count combination coverage.

title: Suggest a sufficient synthetic row count — suggest_min_rows
text: list : n Suggested integer row count. rationale Human-readable explanation. original_n Original row count. combination_count Observed category-combination count (NA). floor Per-column distinct floor used (NA). capped TRUE cap bound suggestion. reduced TRUE suggestion original count.

title: Suggest a sufficient synthetic row count — suggest_min_rows
text: rule (coverage-based) : small inputs (fewer threshold rows, default 1000) original row count kept — nothing gain reducing. Otherwise suggestion number observed cross-column category combinations, capped cap (default 5000) avoid suggesting millions rows wide data, floored largest per-column distinct count every level remains representable. suggestion never exceeds original row count. Continuous columns covered preserving min/max (already handled synthesis engine); raise suggested count.

title: Suggest a sufficient synthetic row count — suggest_min_rows
code: p <- profile_data(datasets::iris) suggest_min_rows(p) #> $n #> [1] 150 #>  #> $rationale #> [1] "Original is small (150 rows); synthesizing the same number." #>  #> $original_n #> [1] 150 #>  #> $combination_count #> [1] NA #>  #> $floor #> [1] NA #>  #> $capped #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $reduced #> [1] FALSE #>

title: Create a synthesis specification — synth_spec
text: Builds synthesis specification purpose preset optional user overrides. specification records synthesis parameters required engine, check engine availability - done synthesize_data().

title: Create a synthesis specification — synth_spec
code: synth_spec(   purpose,   level = NULL,   n = NULL,   roles = NULL,   privacy = NULL,   name_strategy = NULL,   seed = NULL,   engine = NULL,   acknowledge_risk = FALSE,   ... )

title: Create a synthesis specification — synth_spec
text: purpose Character. One "demo", "development", "analytics". NULL, synthesize_data() derives engine objective. level Character NULL. Synthesis level: "schema" "marginal". NULL, derived preset. n Integer NULL. Number rows synthesize. NULL, defaults nrow(original) synthesis time. roles dataganger_roles object NULL. Column role assignments. privacy dataganger_privacy_check object NULL. stage == "pre", flags harden defaults (e.g. IDs dropped, free text removed). name_strategy Character NULL. synthetic column names handled: "preserve" keeps original column names, "generic" replaces neutral names (var1, var2, ...), "dictionary_only" anonymizes names records mapping exported data dictionary. NULL, derived preset. seed Integer NULL. Reproducibility seed. Fixes random draw spec data reproduce exact synthetic output. engine Character NULL. Optional explicit synthesis engine: "internal"/"marginal" synthesizes column distribution (fast, dependency-free, ignores cross-column relationships), "synthpop" models columns conditionally correlations joint structure preserved (higher fidelity, needs synthpop package). NULL, synthesize_data() derives engine objective. acknowledge_risk Logical. Required TRUE purpose = "analytics". ... Additional decision parameters passed spec list. settings exposed Synthesis Settings app: preserve_correlations — strongly cross-variable relationships retained ("none", "moderate", "high"). coarsen_dates — logical; round dates (e.g. month year) exact event date single individual. merge_rare — logical; combine infrequent category values "" group reduce re-identification risk. rare_level_min_n — integer; category values seen fewer many times count rare (merged suppressed). free_text_strategy — free-text columns treated (e.g. "drop", "generic"); usually set purpose preset. preserve_missingness — closely reproduce original pattern missing (NA) values ("approx", "exact", "none").

title: Create a synthesis specification — synth_spec
text: S3 object class dataganger_spec (named list).

title: Create a synthesis specification — synth_spec
code: synth_spec(purpose = "demo") #>  #> ── DataGangeR Synthesis Spec ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Purpose  #> "demo" #>  #> ── Level  #> "marginal" #>  #> ── Key settings  #> • Name strategy: "preserve" #> • Coarsen dates: TRUE #> • Merge rare levels: TRUE (min_n = 5) #> • Minimum cell size (k-anonymity): 5 #> • Free text strategy: "drop" #> • Preserve correlations: "low" #> • Preserve missingness: "approx" #> • Engine required: "internal" synth_spec(purpose = "development", n = 200, seed = 42) #> ℹ Development synthesis uses synthpop for correlation-aware output when #>   installed; review privacy warnings before sharing. #>  #> ── DataGangeR Synthesis Spec ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Purpose  #> "development" #>  #> ── Level  #> "marginal" #>  #> ── Target rows  #> 200 #>  #> ── Key settings  #> • Name strategy: "preserve" #> • Coarsen dates: FALSE #> • Merge rare levels: TRUE (min_n = 5) #> • Minimum cell size (k-anonymity): 5 #> • Free text strategy: "drop" #> • Preserve correlations: "moderate" #> • Preserve missingness: "approx" #> • Engine required: "internal" #>  #> ── Seed  #> 42 #> ℹ Relationship-aware synthesis uses synthpop when installed. synth_spec(purpose = "analytics", acknowledge_risk = TRUE) #>  #> ── DataGangeR Synthesis Spec ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Purpose  #> "analytics" #>  #> ── Level  #> "hifi" #>  #> ── Key settings  #> • Name strategy: "preserve" #> • Coarsen dates: FALSE #> • Merge rare levels: FALSE (min_n = 5) #> • Minimum cell size (k-anonymity): 5 #> • Free text strategy: "redact" #> • Preserve correlations: "high" #> • Preserve missingness: "approx" #> • Engine required: "hifi" #> ! Risk acknowledged by user

title: Synthesize a data double — synthesize_data
text: Creates synthetic copy dataset using specified specification engine. internal engine supports schema-(Level 1) marginal (Level 2) synthesis. optional synthpop engine used objectives request moderate high relationship preservation.

title: Synthesize a data double — synthesize_data
code: synthesize_data(data, spec, roles = NULL, engine = NULL)

title: Synthesize a data double — synthesize_data
text: data data frame synthesize . spec dataganger_spec object synth_spec(). roles Optional; dataganger_roles object detect_roles(). Informs column treatment override spec. engine Character NULL. Engine use: "internal", "marginal" (alias "internal"), "synthpop". NULL, defaults spec$engine derives spec$preserve_correlations.

title: Synthesize a data double — synthesize_data
text: S3 object class dataganger_synthetic, tibble attributes spec, original_dims, seed_used, generated_at.

title: Synthesize a data double — synthesize_data
text: Set options(dataganger.disable_synthpop = TRUE) steer auto-derived synthesis onto internal engine even synthpop installed. intended environments synthpop synthesis undesirable can hang unattended (example continuous integration). explicit engine = "synthpop" request still honoured; objective-derived routing affected.

title: Synthesize a data double — synthesize_data
code: dat <- data.frame(x = 1:5, y = letters[1:5]) spec <- synth_spec(purpose = "demo") syn <- synthesize_data(dat, spec)

title: Temporal synthetic sample data — temporal_sample
text: synthetically generated dataset 365 daily records use sample input DataGangeR Shiny app. Simulates environmental monitoring data across multiple sites. Generated set.seed(42).

title: Temporal synthetic sample data — temporal_sample
code: temporal_sample

title: Temporal synthetic sample data — temporal_sample
text: data frame 365 rows 5 columns: date Measurement date (daily 2023-01-01) site_id Site identifier (SITE_A SITE_E) measurement Numeric measurement value (NAs) temperature Ambient temperature degrees Celsius flagged Logical quality-control flag

title: Temporal synthetic sample data — temporal_sample
text: Synthetically generated via data-raw/temporal_sample.R

title: dataganger 0.4.0
text: Configure redesign around two intrinsic privacy questions. Configure step now classifies column answering two independent questions — point person (identifies: none / combination / direct) sensitive (sensitive) — derives treatment rather asking user pick . two questions shown prominently per-column table. k-anonymity membership now reads axes, column identifying--combination sensitive covered. Numeric quasi-identifiers longer coarsened NA bins. column row Action override column exposing Action (synthesize / pass / drop) data-type overrides directly. Generate step now shows per-column review table (points person?, sensitive?, action, plain-English outcome) choices can verified generating. Objective selection uses single Protection meter, makes development default objective, rewrites per-objective detail panel around consistent dimensions use, values, relationships, identifiers, sensitive / rare data. Synthesis Settings labels human-readable, matching synth_spec() documentation current settings surface. per-column data preview includes filter can inspect one variable time reviewing Configure step. export_synthetic(compact = ) supports two bundle variants: compact app download full CLI / agent bundle.

title: dataganger 0.3.5
text: Generation, comparison, export clarity pass: Generate page now shows read-“Column decisions” snapshot (Configure table final, non-editable values) choices can reviewed generating. New report_issue() helper plus -app Report problem button open pre-filled GitHub issue environment details, without sending anything automatically. engine recap resolves engine actually used (e.g. synthpop (auto)) generation, instead always showing auto. Regenerate button disabled first generation, longer duplicates Generate initial visit. Exact-row-match count moved result stats; redundant verbatim Result box removed. Compare page treats geography columns categorical, get original-vs-synthetic comparison instead skipped. Export page gains generation summary: original rows/columns, many columns synthesized, passed , dropped, final synthetic dimensions. Internal: cancellable-synthesis subprocess longer uses ::: self-reference (clearing R CMD check NOTE); spelling WORDLIST expanded.

title: dataganger 0.3.4
text: Configure page clarity pass: Integer-valued columns now display without spurious decimals (e.g. 127, 127.00) detecting whole-valued numerics, just R integer storage. column-roles table renames “Simulation” Action, folds recommendation inline TYPE control (... (recommended)), drops redundant recommended_role column, adds per-row info tooltip plain-English reason storage type. Setting column’s Action Drop Pass now greys disables TYPE DISCLOSURE selectors longer blocks generation; pass-columns carry “real values - verify sharing” note. Disclosure-detection reason strings rewritten plain English. Collapsible help now uses obvious +/- affordance.

title: dataganger 0.3.3
text: pkgload removed Suggests (uses .__DEVTOOLS__ namespace check directly). CRAN-readiness: cran-comments.md refreshed accurate 0/0/2 NOTE explanations. Added pkgdown site generation via GitHub Actions (_pkgdown.yml + workflows). Planning artifacts (docs/superpowers/, todo.md) migrated package root.

title: dataganger 0.3.2
text: detect_roles() now conservative: auto-assigns disclosure role confident (clear direct identifier, known-sensitive column name). columns left unselected rather defaulted quasi-identifier. fixes root cause 100%-NA synthetic output: measures, counts, dates, low-cardinality categoricals longer silently treated quasi-identifiers. Configure page now requires explicit disclosure role every column generating. live counter shows many still unselected. None valid explicit choice; empty . k-anonymity fires columns user marks quasi-identifiers. max_suppress_frac feasibility backstop retained defense--depth. CLI: spec YAML accepts disclosure_roles: mapping (column -> role) disclosure decisions reproducible command line.

title: dataganger 0.3.1
text: Bug fix — CUSUM hang (Bug 5): Synthesis longer hangs datasets character-stored date columns (e.g. “Jun 8, 2019”) high-cardinality character columns. root cause two-fold: (1) character date strings classified “unknown” passed synthpop 2000+-level factor, causing CART enumerate billions split candidates; (2) even moderate-cardinality character columns (>20 distinct values) used CART predictors trigger 2^(k-1) blowup k=34. Fix: detect_roles() now detects date strings (ISO, “Mon DD YYYY”, MM/DD/YY) via regex classifies “date”; new synthpop_bridge_cols() function excludes character column >20 distinct values synthpop’s CART, synthesizes independently via marginal engine, stitches back output original column order. Generation CUSUM test file (41 k rows, 14 columns) now completes 10 seconds 50-row 5000-row target sizes. P1 — Configure busy indicator: Upload page now shows “Profiling data…” / “Detecting column roles…” progress bar app analyses uploaded file, users know app working rather frozen. P2 — Row count first: Row count (n) input now first item Configure advanced-settings panel. P3 — Role-reactive row suggestion: suggest_min_rows() gains data parameter; called data roles, recomputes coverage estimate columns still synthesized. Dropping ID excluded column now immediately lowers suggested row count Configure page. P4 — Case IDs render character: columns detected ID candidates coerced character data-panel preview, numeric case ID displays “1078541” instead “1,078,541.00”. P5 — Column summary stats: Configure page now shows per-column summary section synthesis settings. Continuous columns get min / Q1 / median / Q3 / max / mean / SD table; categorical columns get top-5 frequency table counts percentages. P6 — Generation progress bar + timer: synthesis running, Generation page displays MM:SS elapsed-time counter animated progress bar. update every second.

title: dataganger 0.3.0
text: Cancellable background synthesis: Shiny Generation step now runs synthesize -> compare -> privacy pipeline background process (via callr), app stays responsive Cancel button can stop long stuck run. Falls back synchronous -process generation callr unavailable package dev-loaded. dataganger.synthesis_async option forces deterministic synchronous path tests CI. Coverage-based row-count suggestion: profile_data() now carries cross-column coverage (distinct joint combinations categorical columns plus largest per-column level count), new suggest_min_rows() function turns sufficient synthetic row count - capped 5000, floored largest level count, never original. Configuration row-count slider pre-fills suggestion, shows inline hint, warns set coverage floor. Upload step shows coverage-summary card. Diagnostics long-running synthesis: dg_log / dg_timeit emit per-phase progress R console options(dataganger.verbose = TRUE), check_cancel() polls options(dataganger.cancel) column boundaries cooperative cancellation. .onAttach() prints startup hint package version launch app CLI. Free-text detection now head-samples 1000 rows, bounding hot path slow transition Configure wide long-string data. synthesize_marginal() trusts detected role instead recomputing free-text heuristic, removing redundant pass character columns. Comparison step longer shows stale full table first transition: first comparable variable renders correctly without needing click. Configuration step shows inline help disclosure role (None, Direct identifier, Quasi-identifier, Sensitive) short example. callr pkgload added Suggests.

title: dataganger 0.2.2
text: Column Roles step now shows non-blocking notice uploaded data looks like aggregated counts table rather individual records, since disclosure control assumes individual-level microdata. New option dataganger.disable_synthpop: set options(dataganger.disable_synthpop = TRUE) steer objective-derived synthesis onto internal engine even synthpop installed. Intended environments synthpop synthesis undesirable can hang unattended (example continuous integration). explicit engine = "synthpop" request still honoured.

title: dataganger 0.2.1
text: Shiny app interface refinements: Objective step shows purpose’s details selection uses consistent “bars = stronger” meters, including Anonymity meter resistance re-identification. synthesis engine (auto, internal, synthpop) now plain-language explainer. Generation step shows configuration recap (including advanced settings) “Adjust settings” shortcut back Configuration. Comparison step wrapping variable grid clearer original vs. synthetic explainer. Export step downloads single bundle (synthetic data CSV, documentation, comparison report, analysis notebook), optional save--folder sessions run locally. Every bundle now includes analysis.qmd, Quarto report runnable R code reference Python code read original synthetic data compare (summary statistics, distribution plots, DataGangeR’s fidelity metrics). Shiny synthesis spec exposes engine selector (auto / internal / synthpop) inline note whether synthpop installed. Ship agent skill (inst/skills/using-dataganger-bundles/) describing AI agents consume bundle: never access real data, ask human go-ahead touching synthetic data, save work. Fixed interpolate() bundle helper files (e.g. load_data.R) render templates instead shipping literal placeholders. DataGangeR now routes relationship-preserving objectives optional synthpop engine installed. Please cite: Nowok B, Raab GM, Dibben C (2016). “synthpop: Bespoke Creation Synthetic Data R.” Journal Statistical Software, 74(11), 1-26. doi:10.18637/jss.v074.i11 Initial CRAN-ready scaffold.
